lede: A look at December 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ella Gross and Brett Williams (footballer, born 1987), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United Russia
events.desc: The United Russia political party was founded.

events.title: World AIDS Day
events.desc: World AIDS Day is proclaimed worldwide by the UN member states.

events.title: Croatian Spring
events.desc: Purge of Croatian Spring leaders starts in Yugoslavia at the meeting of the League of Communists at the Karađorđevo estate.

events.title: Vietnam War draft
events.desc: Vietnam War: The first draft lottery in the United States is held since World War II.

events.title: Fiorello La Guardia
events.desc: World War II: Fiorello La Guardia, Mayor of New York City and Director of the Office of Civilian Defense, signs Administrative Order 9, creating the Civil Air Patrol.

events.title: Sergei Kirov
events.desc: Sergei Kirov is assassinated, paving way for the repressive Great Purge, and Vinnytsia massacre by General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, Joseph Stalin.

events.title: Kingdom of Yugoslavia
events.desc: The Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes (later known as the Kingdom of Yugoslavia) is proclaimed.

events.title: Juan Lavalle
events.desc: Argentine general Juan Lavalle makes a coup against governor Manuel Dorrego, beginning the Decembrist revolution.

events.title: Pedro I of Brazil
events.desc: Pedro I is crowned Emperor of Brazil.

deaths.text: Terry Griffiths, Welsh snooker player and coach
J. B. S. Haldane, English-Indian geneticist and biologist
